findings.description: The prompt_packet field uses a machine-local /Users/... path, so another operator or control plane cannot resolve the measured prompt even though the hash is present.
The packet marks itself deployable and primary-reviewer capable even though g3_signed is false, so it bypasses the human G3 launch gate.
The evidence section leaves run_dir/report/lineage/pareto/trials empty, so a reviewer cannot trace the pinned composition back to measured run records.
The contract leaves trace_destination as TBD and trace_artifact empty, so launch review has neither a live sink nor an explicit JSONL-only waiver.
The contract allows direct GitHub PR comments and exposes GITHUB_TOKEN while g4_signed is false, expanding write authority before the human G4 gate.
The prompt_packet field uses an absolute local filesystem path that other control-plane hosts cannot resolve, making the packet non-portable and likely to fail on import.
Packet claims deployable = true but gates.g3_signed is false; an unsigned packet must not be marked deployable.
Packet sets primary_reviewer_allowed = true while gates.g3_signed is false; an unsigned packet should not be permitted to act as primary reviewer.
Every evidence pointer (run_dir, report, lineage, pareto, trials) is an empty string, so the pinned composition hash cannot be validated against any produced artifacts.
g3_signed is false, meaning the launch packet is unsigned and lacks the required G3 approval to proceed.
g3_signed is false, so the deployment is unsigned and cannot pass the required approval gate.
trace_destination is set to the placeholder TBD rather than a live trace sink, leaving the agent without runtime observability.
trace_artifact is empty and no JSONL-only waiver is declared, so there is no fallback trace evidence when the live sink is unavailable.
